lede: A look at October 3 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Noah Schnapp and Anthony Le Tallec, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Bengali language
events.desc: Bengali, Assamese, Marathi, Pali and Prakrit are accorded the Classical language status by the Government of India

events.title: 2013 Lampedusa migrant shipwreck
events.desc: At least 360 migrants are killed when their boat sinks near the Italian island of Lampedusa.

events.title: Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008
events.desc: The Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008 for the U.S. financial system is signed by President George W. Bush.

events.title: Nadine Gordimer
events.desc: Nadine Gordimer is announced as the winner of the Nobel Prize in Literature.

events.title: 1981 Irish hunger strike
events.desc: The hunger strike at the Maze Prison in Northern Ireland ends after seven months and ten deaths.

events.title: 1963 Honduran coup d'état
events.desc: A violent coup in Honduras begins two decades of military rule.

events.title: Operation Hurricane
events.desc: The United Kingdom successfully tests a nuclear weapon in the Montebello Islands, Western Australia, to become the world's third nuclear power.

events.title: First Battle of Maryang-san
events.desc: Korean War: The First Battle of Maryang San pits Commonwealth troops against communist Chinese troops.

events.title: Kintpuash
events.desc: Chief Kintpuash and companions are hanged for their part in the Modoc War of northern California.

events.title: Muhammad VII of Granada
events.desc: Muhammed VII becomes the twelfth sultan of the Emirate of Granada.

births.name: Edward Oliver LeBlanc
births.line: Dominican lawyer and politician, 1st Premier of Dominica (died 2004)

deaths.text: Sergei Belov, Russian basketball player and coach
William Steig, American sculptor, author, and illustrator
Vince DiMaggio, American baseball player and manager
Robert Barclay, Scottish theologian and politician, 2nd Governor of East Jersey

observances.text: Christian feast day: Ewald the Black and Ewald the Fair
Christian feast day: John Raleigh Mott (Episcopal Church)
